text: A bad workman blames his tools.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Be nice to geek’s, you’ll probably end up working for one
author: Bill Gates

text: Be undeniably good. No marketing effort or social media buzzword can be a substitute for that.

text: By working faithfully eight hours a day you may eventually get to be boss and work twelve hours a day.

text: Careful thinking and hard work will solve nearly all your problems. Try and see for yourself.
author: Ullery

text: Don't waste your life in doubts and fears: Spend yourself on the work before you, well assured that the right performance of this hour's duties will be the best preparation for the hours or ages that follow it.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Entrepreneurship is neither a science nor an art. It is a practice.
author: Peter Drucker, management consultant, educator, and author.

text: Every day I get up and look through the Forbes list of the richest people in America. If I’m not there, I go to work.

text: Have the end in mind and every day make sure your working towards it

text: Hire character. Train skill.

text: I have not failed. I’ve just found 10,000 ways that won’t work

text: I have not failed. I’ve just found 10,000 ways that won’t work.
author: Thomas Edison, inventor.

text: If hard work is the key to success, most people would rather pick the lock.

text: If I find 10,000 ways something won’t work, I haven’t failed. I am not discouraged, because every wrong attempt discarded is often a step forward.
author: Thomas Edison

text: Most 'impossible' goals can be met simply by breaking them down into bite-size chunks, writing them down, believing them, and then going full speed ahead as if they were routine.

text: Nothing will work unless you do.

text: Opportunity is missed by most people because it is dressed in overalls and looks like work.

text: The only place where success comes before work is in the dictionary.

text: The successful warrior is the average man, with laser-like focus.

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, therefore, is not an act but a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: You cannot depend on your eyes when your imagination is out of focus.
author: Mark Twain

author: General Douglas MacArthur

text: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.

text: Assume you have 90 seconds with a new user before they decide to use your app or delete it.

text: Do not wait to strike until the iron is hot; but make it hot by striking.

text: Stay self-funded as long as possible.
author: Garrett Camp, co-founder of Stumbleupon

text: Take risks and you'll get the payoffs. Learn from your mistakes until you succeed. It's that simple.
author: Bobby Flay

text: Take time to deliberate; but when the time for action arrives, stop thinking and go in.
author: Andrew Jackson

text: Teachers open the door. You enter by yourself.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: The art of being wise is knowing what to overlook.
author: William James

text: The art of leadership is saying no, not yes. It is very easy to say yes.
author: Tony Blair

text: The best time to plant a tree is twenty years ago. The second best time is now.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
author: Chinese proverb

text: The best use of life is to spend it for something that outlasts it
author: William James

text: The best way out is always through.
author: Robert Frost

text: The critical ingredient is getting off your butt and doing something.
author: Nolan Bushnell

text: The critical ingredient is getting off your butt and doing something. It’s as simple as that. A lot of people have ideas, but there are few who decide to do something about them now. Not tomorrow. Not next week. But today. The true entrepreneur is a doer not a dreamer.
author: Nolan Bushnell, founder of Atari

text: The critical ingredient is getting off your butt and doing something. It’s as simple as that. A lot of people have ideas, but there are few who decide to do something about them now. Not tomorrow. Not next week. But today. The true entrepreneur is a doer, not a dreamer.
author: Nolan Bushnell, entrepreneur.

text: The diamond cannot be polished without friction, nor the man perfected without trials.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: The difference between a boss and a leader: a boss says, ‘Go!’ -a leader says, ‘Let’s go!’.
author: E. M. Kelly

text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts; therefore guard accordingly.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The longer you’re not taking action the more money you’re losing
author: Carrie Wilkerson

text: The man who does not work for the love of work but only for money is likely to neither make money nor find much fun in life.
author: Charles M. Schwab, American steel magnate

text: The more difficulties one has to encounter, within and without, the more significant and the higher in inspiration his life will be.
author: Horace Bushnell

text: The only journey is the one within.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow will be our doubts of today.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Agatha Christie

text: The significance of a man is not in what he attains but in what he longs to attain.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: The surest way not to fail is to determine to succeed.
author: Richard Brinsley Sheridan

text: The vision must be followed by the venture. It is not enough to stare up the steps – we must step up the stairs.
author: Vance Hayner

text: The whole problem with the world is that fools and fanatics are always so certain of themselves, but wiser people so full of doubts.
author: Bertrand Russell

text: There is no happiness except in the realization that we have accomplished something.
author: Henry Ford

text: There is only one way to avoid criticism: Do nothing, say nothing, and be nothing.
author: Aristotle, Greek philosopher

text: There’s an entrepreneur right now, scared to death, making excuses, saying, ‘It’s not the right time just yet.’ There’s no such thing as a good time. Get out of your garage and go take a chance, and start your business.
author: Kevin Plank, CEO of Under Armour

text: There’s no shortage of remarkable ideas, what’s missing is the will to execute them.
author: Seth Godin, author and speaker

text: To reach a port, we must sail
author: sail, not tie at anchor

text: To win without risk is to triumph without glory
author: Corneille

text: Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things that you didn’t do than by the ones you did do.
author: Mark Twain

text: Twenty years from now, you will be more disappointed by the things that you didn’t do than by the ones you did do, so throw off the bowlines, sail away from safe harbor, catch the trade winds in your sails. Explore, Dream, Discover.
author: Mark Twain, author

text: User experience is everything. It always has been, but it’s undervalued and underinvested in. If you don’t know user-centered design, study it. Hire people who know it. Obsess over it. Live and breathe it. Get your whole company on board.
author: Evan Williams, co-founder of Twitter

text: We can do anything we want to do if we stick to it long enough.
author: Helen Keller

text: We make a living by what we get. But we make a life by what we give.
author: Winston Churchhill

text: What do you need to start a business? Three simple things: know your product better than anyone, know your customer, and have a burning desire to succeed.
author: Dave Thomas, founder of Wendy’s

text: Whatever the mind can conceive and believe, the mind can achieve
author: Dr. Napoleon Hill

text: Whatever the mind can conceive and believe, the mind can achieve.
author: Dr. Napoleon Hill, author of Think and Grow Rich.

text: When times are bad is when the real entrepreneurs emerge.
author: Robert Kiyosaki, author of Rich Dad Poor Dad

text: When you go to buy, don’t show your silver
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Whether you think you can or you can’t, you’re right.
author: Henry Ford

text: Wonder what your customer really wants? Ask. Don’t tell.
author: Lisa Stone, co-founder of BlogHer

text: Worry about being better; bigger will take care of itself. Think one customer at a time and take care of each one the best way you can.
author: Gary Comer, founder of Land’s End

text: Years teach us more than books.
author: Berthold Auerbach

text: Yesterday’s home runs don’t win today’s games.
author: Babe Ruth

text: You can say anything to anyone, but how you say it will determine how they will react.
author: John Rampton, entrepreneur and investor

text: You don’t buy a nice car and get rich you get rich and buy a nice car
author: Unknown

text: - In the case of a person who dwells on objects, there arises attachment to them. From attachment grows hankering, and from hankering springs anger.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: 'I have gained this today; I will acquire this desired object. This is in hand; and this wealth will also come to me.'
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: 'This I have gained today, and this desire I shall attain. This wealth is mine, and this will also be mine hereafter.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: (a) Indeed, the self seated in Prakrti experiences the Gunas born of Prakrti. (b) Its attachment to these Gunas is the cause of birth in good and evil wombs.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: (a) Its branches extend both above and below, nourished by the Gunas. Their shoots are sense objects. (b) And their secondary roots extend downwards, resulting in actions that bind in the world of humans.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: (a) Salutations to You from before and behind! Salutations to You from all sides, O All!৷৷. (b) ৷৷. O You of infinite prowess and heroic action, which are measureless! You pervade all beings, and therefore, You are all.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: (a) When Rajas is prevalent, one meets with dissolution and is born among those attached to work. (b) Similarly, when Tamas prevails, one who has met with dissolution is born in the womb of beings lacking intelligence.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: (They are): Your venerable self, Bhisma, Karna, and Krpa, who is ever victorious in battle; Asvatthama, Vikarna, Saumadatti, and Jayadratha.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: A knower of Brahman, who is established in Brahman, should have their intellect steady and should not be deluded. They should not get delighted by getting what is desirable, nor become dejected by getting what is undesirable.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: A lamp does not flicker in a windless place"—that is the simile used for the tranquil mind of a yogi who practices yoga.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: A person does not attain freedom from action by abstaining from action; nor does he attain fulfillment merely through renunciation.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: A person who has become free from hankering for all desirable objects is said to be Self-absorbed when the controlled mind rests solely in the Self.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: A person with an evenness of mind discards both good and evil here and now. Therefore, strive for Yoga; for Yoga is skill in action.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: A yogi is higher than those of austerity; they are considered higher even than those of knowledge. The yogi is also higher than those of action. Therefore, O Arjuna, become a yogi.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: A yogi should constantly concentrate his mind by staying in a solitary place, alone, with mind and body controlled, free from expectations, and free from acquisition.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: A yogi, renouncing the fruits of their actions, attains lasting peace. But the unsteady person who is attached to the fruits of their actions, being driven by desire, is bound.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Abandoning attachment and established in Yoga, perform works, viewing success and failure with an even mind. Evenness of mind is said to be Yoga.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Absence of desire for sense-objects, and also absence of egotism, the recognition of evil in birth, death, old age, disease, and sorrow;
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Action done with attachment, O Arjuna, is far inferior to action done with evenness of mind. Seek refuge in evenness of mind; those who act with a motive for results are miserable.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Action is said to be the means for the sage who seeks to climb the heights of Yoga; but when he has climbed the heights of Yoga, tranquility is said to be the means.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Actions are being performed in every way by the Gunas of Prakrti; he whose nature is deluded by egoism, however, thinks, 'I am the doer.'
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Actions do not bind him, O Arjuna, who has renounced them through Karma Yoga and whose doubts have been dispelled by knowledge, and who thus possesses a steady mind.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Actions do not taint Me; for I have no hankering for the results of actions. One who knows Me thus, does not become bound by their actions.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: Agriculture, cattle-breeding, and trade are the duties of the Vaisya, born of their nature. And the duty of a Sudra is one of service, born of their nature.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Alas! We have resolved to commit a great sin, for we are ready to slay our kin out of desire for sovereignty and enjoyment.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: All beings arise from food; food is produced from rain; rain comes from sacrifice; and sacrifice is born of action.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: All these are indeed generous, but I deem the man of knowledge to be My very self; for he, integrated, is devoted solely to Me as the highest end.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: all these are to be regarded as one's own self. Teachers, fathers, sons, grandfathers, uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, and other kinsmen—all these are to be regarded as one's own self.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Among the Vedas, I am the Sama-Veda; among the gods, I am Indra. Among the organs, I am the mind, and I am the intelligence in creatures.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: An everlasting part of Myself, having become the bound self in the world of life, attracts the senses, of which the mind is the sixth, and which abide in Prakriti.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: And he who sees actions being done in various ways by Nature itself, and also the Self as the non-agent, he sees.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: And he who serves Me through the unswerving Yoga of Devotion, he, having gone beyond these qualities, qualifies for becoming Brahman.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: And he who, with unwavering devotion through Yoga, serves Me, he, transcending the Gunas, becomes fit for the state of Brahman.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: And I am seated in the hearts of all. From Me come memory, knowledge, and their loss. I alone am the object to be known through all the Vedas; I am also the originator of the Vedanta, and I Myself am the knower of the Vedas.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: And of the strong, I am the strength that is devoid of passion and attachment. Among creatures, I am desire that is not contrary to righteousness, O scion of the Bharata dynasty.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: And the diligent yogis see this one existing within themselves. The non-discriminating ones who lack self-control, however, do not see this one, even though they are diligent.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: And the steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity, and charity is spoken of as sat (good). And even the action meant for these is, indeed, called as sat.
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: And those who know Me with the Adhibhuta, Adhidaiva, and Adhiyajna, they too, with their minds fixed in meditation, know Me even at the hour of death.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: And unwavering devotion to Me with single-minded concentration; inclination to retreat to a clean place; lack of delight in a crowd of people;
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: Applying his mind in this way, the Yogin of controlled mind, ever attains the peace which is the summit of beatitude and which abides in Me.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Arjuna said, "Having beheld Your human and pleasing form, O Krsna, I am now composed in mind and have been restored to my normal nature."
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Arjuna said, "I desire to know the truth about renunciation and abnegation separately, O Krishna."
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Arjuna said, "If a person who is possessed of faith but has put in only inadequate effort finds his mind wandering away from Yoga, and then fails to attain perfection—what way does he go, O Krishna?"
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Arjuna said, "My delusion has been destroyed, and, by Your grace, O Krsna, I have gained knowledge (Smrti). Freed from doubts, I stand steadfast, and I will fulfill Your will."
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Hrsikesa, it is proper that the world be delighted and attracted by Your praise; that the Raksasas, stricken with fear, run in all directions; and that all the groups of the Siddhas bow down to You."
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Janardana (Krishna), if it is Your opinion that wisdom is superior to action, why do You urge me to take such horrible action, O Kesava?"
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Janardana, having seen this serene human form of Yours, I have now become calm in mind and restored to my own nature."
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Kesava, what is the description of a man of steady wisdom who is self-absorbed? How does he speak, sit, and move about?"
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, if you deem knowledge to be superior to action, why then do you urge me to engage in this terrible deed?"
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, this Yoga of reality, which You have declared, I do not see how it can be steady due to the fickleness of the mind."
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, what goal does one attain who, despite having faith, fails to achieve perfection in Yoga due to lack of diligence, and whose mind becomes deflected from Yoga?"
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, you praise renunciation of actions and, at the same time, Karma-yoga. Please tell me for certain which one is better between these two."
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Krsna, you praise the renunciation of actions, and then you also praise Karma Yoga. Please tell me with certainty which of these is the superior one, leading to the ultimate good.
author: Swami Adidevananda

text: Arjuna said, "O Madhusudana (Krsna), this Yoga that You have spoken of as sameness, I do not see its steady continuance due to the restlessness of the mind."
author: Swami Gambirananda

text: Arjuna said, "This delusion of mine has departed as a result of that speech which is most secret and pertains to the Self, uttered by You for my benefit."
author: Swami Gambirananda
